title: Module 2: Fairness & Bias Assessment Schema
description: Machine-readable audit framework for detecting discriminatory outcomes in AI-driven financial decisions

evaluation_criteria.description: AI-driven financial decisions (expense approvals, credit scoring, vendor selection) exhibit no systematic bias across protected demographics
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Analyze approval rates across gender/race/age groups; test for disparate impact
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: Demographic parity within ±5% across protected groups, equalized odds metrics meet thresholds, training data representativeness confirmed

statistical_requirements.sample_size_per_group: 1000 transactions per protected group or entire population if smaller

pass_thresholds.equalized_odds: False positive rate and false negative rate within ±5% across groups

evidence_required.representativeness_standard: Training data demographics within ±10% of population distribution; justify if not (e.g., intentional oversampling)

scoring.calculation: Average of pass/fail across all required metrics

severity_mapping.justification: Systematic bias in financial decisions creates regulatory penalties, litigation risk, and reputation damage
financial_impact_categories: regulatory_penalties (fair lending violations, EU AI Act fines)

regulatory_mapping.nist_ai_rmf: MEASURE-2.10, 2.11 - Fair with Harmful Bias Managed not achieved
regulatory_mapping.eu_ai_act: Article 10 violation if training data unrepresentative

evaluation_criteria.description: Ongoing monitoring system detecting emerging bias in production AI systems
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review bias monitoring dashboards, alert configurations, response procedures
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: Bias metrics monitored monthly minimum, alerts configured, documented response to alerts

evidence_required.alert_threshold: Demographic parity exceeds ±5% or disparate impact outside 0.8-1.25

severity_mapping.justification: Without ongoing bias monitoring, organization cannot detect emerging discriminatory patterns

evaluation_criteria.description: Documented procedures for bias mitigation when bias detected
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review bias mitigation playbook, test historical bias incident responses
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: Documented mitigation procedures exist, procedures tested annually, bias incidents resolved within SLA

severity_mapping.justification: Without bias mitigation procedures, detected bias may not be remediated timely

interdependency_triggers.action: Analyze whether accuracy varies across demographic groups
investigation_questions: Does model predict accurately for Group A but poorly for Group B?
Is this fair but uniformly inaccurate OR accurate but biased?
interdependency_triggers.rationale: Distinguishes 'fair but inaccurate model' (needs retraining) from 'accurate but biased model' (needs bias mitigation)
